intro: Wwise is freemium with a free tier; LÖVE is free and open source. LÖVE: favored for game jams and indie projects. Wwise: Professional interactive audio middleware by Audiokinetic. Industry standard for adaptive music and sound design in AAA games, with deep Unity and Unreal integration. LÖVE fits solo developers and learners who want Lua, code-first, desktop-only workflows. Wwise fits AAA and mid-tier studios needing top-tier adaptive and spatial audio. On our rubric LÖVE scores 4/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency, while Wwise scores 5/5 and 3/5. The honest trade-off: LÖVE's main drawback — No built-in editor — code everything by hand; Wwise's — Steeper learning curve than FMOD for audio teams. One point in LÖVE's favour: Minimal API — you can learn it in an afternoon. For Wwise: The AAA audio middleware gold standard. LÖVE is explicitly not the right pick for teams needing editors, mobile-first targets, or console publishing. Wwise is not aimed at hobby projects and small mobile games where Unity audio covers the need.
quick_take: LÖVE is for solo developers; Wwise is for AAA; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose LÖVE if solo developers and learners who want Lua, code-first, desktop-only workflows, and if you want to avoid recurring license costs and are willing to self-host.
verdict_b: Choose Wwise if AAA and mid-tier studios needing top-tier adaptive and spatial audio, and if you accept tighter coupling in exchange for the managed surface.

faq.a: Yes on list price: LÖVE is free and open source, while Wwise runs on freemium pricing. Keep in mind that LÖVE shifts costs to hosting, ops, and engineering time. Total cost of ownership can flip depending on your team's capacity.

faq.q: Can I migrate from LÖVE to Wwise?
faq.a: Migrating out of LÖVE is straightforward — it's rated low lock-in (OSS Lua game framework). The harder question is how deeply you commit to Wwise afterward, since it sits at medium lock-in (Industry-standard, portable projects). Plan the forward cost, not just the exit.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, LÖVE or Wwise?
faq.a: Wwise scores higher in our rubric (5/5 vs 4/5 for LÖVE). Transparency is 3/5 for Wwise and 5/5 for LÖVE. DX scores are rubric-based, not benchmarks, so evaluate against your own toolchain.

faq.q: Is Wwise a good alternative to LÖVE?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — Wwise is a plausible alternative for many LÖVE use cases. It fits best when AAA and mid-tier studios needing top-tier adaptive and spatial audio. Skip it if hobby projects and small mobile games where Unity audio covers the need.
